title: He’s running the Boston Marathon to support neglected and at-risk kids
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Sean Meehan is participating in the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Meehan runs to support The Home For Little Wanderers.
The charity assists neglected and at-risk children.

title: The Boston Marathon didn’t make this list of best marathons in America. Do you think it should have?
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Boston Marathon was not included in USA Today's 2026 10Best Readers' Choice Awards for best marathons in the U.S.
The top marathon spots went to the Detroit Free Press Marathon and the Cincinnati Flying Pig Marathon among others.
Despite its historical and iconic status, the Boston Marathon was overlooked in the selection of top marathons.

title: Everything you need to know about camping at Massachusetts state parks this season
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Massachusetts DCR's 2026 camping season runs April to November with sites available at over 30 parks.
Reservations for DCR parks can be made up to four months in advance, but walk-ins are not allowed.
Camping costs vary based on location and residency, with higher fees for out-of-state campers.

title: Roman Anthony: Red Sox 2-7 start is ‘unacceptable to the fans’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Red Sox have worst start since 2019, losing seven of first nine games, including a sweep by Houston.
Outfielder Roman Anthony calls 2-7 start 'unacceptable' for fans and team standards after loss to Padres.
Boston ranks 17th in batting average and tied for third-most defensive errors in MLB with nine.

title: Is this really Boston’s most annoying escalator?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: A Reddit post claims the Prudential-to-Copley Place escalator is out of service about 75% of the time.
Readers debate on Reddit, nominating other escalators at Porter Square, Courthouse Station, and Logan Airport as more frustrating.
Boston.com invites reader input on the region's most annoying escalators for an upcoming feature article.

title: NFL analyst predicts two possible Patriots draft trades
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots are set to pick 31st overall with 11 selections in the 2026 NFL Draft.
ESPN analyst Bill Barnwell suggests Patriots may trade up with Browns for the 24th overall pick.
Another predicted trade involves Patriots trading down with Jets, gaining 33rd and 44th picks.

title: Painter claiming injury at Bill Belichick’s house sues him over alleged unsafe work site
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Painter Andrew Jackson is suing ex-Patriots coach Bill Belichick for $285,436 over an alleged unsafe work site injury.
The incident occurred on June 21, 2024, at Belichick's home at 45 Fair St. in Nantucket, where Jackson fell.
Jackson claims unsafe conditions caused his fall, leading to serious ankle injury, pain, and financial losses.

title: Woman shot and killed by police in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A woman was fatally shot by police during a domestic disturbance response in Northfield, New Hampshire, on Monday.
The incident was the third police-related deadly force case in New England since Saturday, officials reported.
Authorities have not released the identities of the woman or the officer involved in the shooting.

title: Man accused of assaulting two workers, stealing car during 3 separate trips to Dedham gas station
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: John Laurent, 35, of West Roxbury, arrested for two assaults and car theft at Dedham gas station.
Police suspect Laurent committed separate crimes during three visits to Sam’s Express on Washington St.
Laurent is charged with assault, trespassing, and vehicle larceny; arraignment set for Monday in Dedham.

title: Mass. business confidence dips back into pessimism
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts business confidence fell to 47 in March due to surging energy prices and the war in Iran.
The AIM Business Confidence Index survey includes over 140 employers and uses a 100-point scale, with 50 as neutral.
U.S. job growth rose with 178,000 new jobs in March, while unemployment remained stable at 4.3%.
